key_insight: ACE potentials can capture screw-dislocation glide in BCC refractory metals much more faithfully than prior classical or many ML potentials, but only when the training database and element-specific energetics are carefully designed.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable atomistic predictions of flow stress, glide planes, and kink-pair barriers in refractory metals relevant to high-temperature structural applications.
method: Extended an existing DFT database for V, Nb, Ta, Mo, and W.
Trained an array of atomic cluster expansion (ACE) interatomic potentials.
Benchmarked dislocation properties against DFT, including Peierls barriers and kink-pair activation enthalpies.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-DFT accuracy for Mo and W, substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta, and reliable kink-pair enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not enough to guarantee correct dislocation glide, so validation must include glide-plane behavior and broader defect energetics.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
